proxyDesc: Route downloads, update checks and translation services through a proxy
downloadEndpointsDesc: Customize the mirror / proxy URLs used to download models and components. Changes take effect immediately, no app update required. Leave blank to use defaults.

githubProxyPrefix.label: GitHub proxy prefix
githubProxyPrefix.hint: Prepended to full GitHub links to speed up GitHub Release downloads (no trailing slash needed), e.g. https://gh-proxy.com

huggingFaceMirror.label: HuggingFace mirror
huggingFaceMirror.hint: HuggingFace mirror URL for downloading Whisper / FasterWhisper / FunASR models (include protocol, no trailing slash), e.g. https://hf-mirror.com

modelScopeBase.label: ModelScope site
modelScopeBase.hint: ModelScope site URL for downloading Qwen models, e.g. https://modelscope.cn

gitcodeBase.label: GitCode mirror
gitcodeBase.hint: GitCode site URL for downloading engine / accelerator binaries, e.g. https://gitcode.com

useLocalWhisperTip: Please make sure you have Whisper installed locally and can run the 'whisper' command in terminal
whisperCommandTip: Available variables: ${audioFile}, ${whisperModel}, ${srtFile}, ${sourceLanguage}, ${outputDir}. These will be replaced with actual values when executing.
restoreDefaultsDescription: Reset all settings to their default values, including translation settings and system settings. This action cannot be undone.
batchSizeTip: Due to token limits of different models, you can control how many subtitles to translate at once by splitting the full text. This controls the maximum number of subtitles per translation batch.
tempDirTip: The directory where temporary audio files are stored during processing
maxContextTip: Controls the context length when Whisper processes audio. Default is -1. If you experience subtitle repetition issues, try setting this to 0.
useCustomTempDir: Use custom temporary directory, please avoid using Chinese paths, and manually clean up the custom temporary directory to avoid accidental deletion of files
checkUpdateOnStartupTip: Automatically check for new version updates when the application starts
preventSleepDuringTask: Prevent sleep while tasks are running
preventSleepDuringTaskTip: Keep the system awake only while transcription or subtitle merge tasks are running, then restore normal sleep behavior automatically.
preventSleepDuringTaskEnabled: Sleep prevention enabled for running tasks
preventSleepDuringTaskDisabled: Sleep prevention disabled for running tasks
closeActionTip: Smart: keep running in the background while tasks are active, quit when idle. Keep running in background: the app always stays running after closing the window (quit with Cmd+Q or Dock right-click). Quit the app: closing the window quits; if tasks are running you'll be asked to confirm first.
vadSettingsDesc: Tune voice activity detection sensitivity; applies to every engine that runs VAD. Whether VAD is on is decided per task by the "Subtitle effect" tier. Defaults are best practice.
vadSensitivityNote: Whether VAD is on/off is decided automatically per engine by the "Subtitle effect" tier. Here you only tune its sensitivity: stricter = cleaner (may drop words), looser = more complete (may keep noise).
enableVadTip: Voice Activity Detection can improve transcription accuracy by automatically detecting speech segments and filtering out silence
vadBuiltinHint: Built-in whisper engine: turning VAD off gives finer segmentation and more accurate text (recommended for short/medium files); on suits long audio and strict "no subtitle during silence". Keep it on for other engines (e.g. faster-whisper).
vadThresholdTip: Speech detection threshold (0.0-1.0). Higher values require stronger speech signals
vadMinSpeechDurationTip: Minimum duration for a segment to be considered speech
vadMinSilenceDurationTip: Minimum duration of silence to split speech segments
vadMaxSpeechDurationTip: Maximum duration for a single speech segment. Use 0 for unlimited
vadSpeechPadTip: Padding added around detected speech segments
vadSamplesOverlapTip: Overlap ratio between consecutive audio analysis windows (0.0-1.0)

gpuAcceleration.title: GPU Acceleration
gpuAcceleration.tabDesc: Manage GPU acceleration packages and run mode to speed up transcription
gpuAcceleration.noUpdates: All acceleration packs are up to date
gpuAcceleration.confirmDeleteDesc: Are you sure you want to delete CUDA {{version}} acceleration pack? This action cannot be undone.
gpuAcceleration.customAddonSet: Custom addon set, restart the app to take effect
gpuAcceleration.customAddonTip: You can download acceleration packs from the link below. After downloading, extract the addon.node file.
gpuAcceleration.customAddonDllTip: Tip: If the pack includes DLL/SO dependencies, ensure they are in the same directory as addon.node
gpuAcceleration.crashTip: If the app crashes after enabling acceleration, try switching to a different version or disabling GPU acceleration
gpuAcceleration.modeAutoDesc: Automatically picks the best available acceleration method with graceful fallback — transcription always completes
gpuAcceleration.modeGpuOnlyDesc: Force GPU; tasks fail if no GPU acceleration can be loaded
gpuAcceleration.gpuOnlyWarning: In GPU-only mode, tasks will fail when GPU is unavailable
gpuAcceleration.macAccelAutoDesc: Prefer CoreML (Apple Neural Engine): lower power and heat; first use of a model requires system compilation, which may take a while
gpuAcceleration.macAccelMetalDesc: Always use the Metal GPU: instant start, stable, no compilation wait, works with all models
gpuAcceleration.macAccelTip: Quantized models (q5/q8) have no CoreML version and always use Metal. The first CoreML run of a model requires system compilation — medium/large models may take minutes to tens of minutes, during which progress appears paused.
gpuAcceleration.macAccelIntelNote: Intel-based Macs support CPU transcription only; CoreML / Metal acceleration is not available
gpuAcceleration.statusAutoReady: Ready · acceleration method will be chosen on first transcription run
gpuAcceleration.statusGpuOnlyPending: GPU-only mode · awaiting first transcription to load acceleration pack
gpuAcceleration.upgradeHint: {{gpu}} detected. Upgrading to a CUDA pack can boost performance by ~10-30%
gpuAcceleration.fullEditionDesc: Includes CUDA runtime, works out of the box
gpuAcceleration.liteEditionDesc: Smaller download, requires CUDA Toolkit on this machine
gpuAcceleration.liteRequiresToolkit: Lite edition requires CUDA Toolkit installed locally
gpuAcceleration.cudaVersionGuide: The CUDA version is the runtime bundled in the pack — it must not exceed what your GPU driver supports (see Diagnostics). We pre-select the best match for your driver; keep the default unless you hit compatibility issues.
gpuAcceleration.cudaVersionOlderHint: Older pack — choose manually for legacy drivers or compatibility troubleshooting
gpuAcceleration.remoteVersionsFallback: Could not fetch online version list; showing built-in list
gpuAcceleration.cudaNotApplicable: No NVIDIA GPU detected, CUDA unavailable
gpuAcceleration.packageTypeHintFull: Full: bundles the CUDA runtime libraries. Larger download (hundreds of MB) but works out of the box with no extra setup — best compatibility, recommended for most users.
gpuAcceleration.packageTypeHintLite: Lite: contains only the acceleration plugin itself. Small download, but requires a CUDA Toolkit installed on your system to provide the runtime libraries.
gpuAcceleration.packageTypeHintAuto: Auto-selected on first download: Lite if a local CUDA Toolkit is detected, otherwise Full. You can switch anytime here after installation.
gpuAcceleration.linuxVulkanHint: On Linux, if the Vulkan runtime is missing: sudo apt install libvulkan1
gpuAcceleration.updateDriverHint: If this machine has a discrete GPU but acceleration is off, try updating the GPU driver
gpuAcceleration.customAddonSwitchHint: A custom addon overrides built-in acceleration choices. Clear the custom path to switch between Vulkan and CUDA.

recommendReason.cudaNotSupported: No NVIDIA GPU detected — CUDA acceleration unavailable
recommendReason.cudaVersionTooOld: GPU driver supports a CUDA version too old for any available pack
recommendReason.maxCudaUnknown: Could not parse driver CUDA version — recommending highest available pack
recommendReason.toolkitInstalled: CUDA Toolkit detected locally — lite pack recommended
recommendReason.toolkitMissing: No CUDA Toolkit detected — full pack recommended (bundled runtime)

configImportExportDescription: Export or import app configuration (including provider settings and system settings). Exported files are encrypted and require a password.
enterPasswordForExportDescription: The exported config file will be encrypted with this password. Please remember it — you will need it when importing.
enterPasswordForImportDescription: Enter the password that was set when the config was exported.
importSuccess: Configuration imported successfully. Some settings may require a restart to take effect.
advancedSettingsDesc: Geek options: local whisper command and VAD fine-tuning — defaults are best practice
modelsPathMoved: Model storage path is now managed in the Resource Hub
gpuAccelerationMoved: GPU acceleration settings are now in Resource Hub → Acceleration
